questions.question: What is cloud computing?
options: Using a computer without internet access
Delivering computing services such as servers, storage, databases, and networking over the internet
Installing software only on local machines

questions.question: Which of the following is a key characteristic of cloud computing?
Why do businesses adopt cloud computing?
options: To gain scalability, flexibility, and cost efficiency

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a cloud computing service?
options: Purchasing a physical server for a data center
Using AWS EC2 to launch virtual machines on demand

questions.question: What does "on-demand" mean in cloud computing?
options: Resources are available only during business hours
Resources can be provisioned whenever needed without waiting for hardware purchases
Resources are available only after approval from the provider

questions.question: Which cloud computing benefit allows applications to handle increased traffic automatically?
Which statement best describes the pay-as-you-go pricing model?
options: Users pay a fixed amount regardless of usage
Users pay only for the cloud resources they consume

questions.question: A startup launches a website and expects traffic to increase rapidly. Why is cloud computing beneficial?
options: It allows resources to scale as demand grows without major hardware investments
It eliminates the need for security

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT typically considered a cloud computing benefit?
Which statement best summarizes cloud computing?
options: Cloud computing delivers IT resources over the internet with flexibility, scalability, and pay-as-you-go pricing
Cloud computing requires organizations to own all infrastructure
Cloud computing is only used by large enterprises

questions.question: What are IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS in cloud computing?
options: Cloud service delivery models that define how resources are provided to users

questions.question: What does IaaS stand for?
Which AWS service is a common example of IaaS?
What does PaaS stand for?
Which of the following is an example of PaaS?
What does SaaS stand for?
Which of the following is an example of SaaS?
Which service model provides the highest level of user control over infrastructure?
Which service model requires the least infrastructure management from the customer?
Which statement best summarizes IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS?
options: IaaS provides infrastructure, PaaS provides development platforms, and SaaS provides complete software solutions
All three models provide identical services
SaaS offers more infrastructure control than IaaS

questions.question: What is a cloud deployment model?
options: A method for training AI models
A way of defining where cloud infrastructure is deployed and who can access it

questions.question: What is a Public Cloud?
options: A cloud environment owned and operated by a third-party provider and shared among multiple customers
A cloud environment used by only one organization
A cloud deployed without internet access

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a Public Cloud provider?
What is a Private Cloud?
options: A cloud environment dedicated to a single organization
A public cloud shared by everyone
A cloud service available only during business hours

questions.question: Why might an organization choose a Private Cloud?
options: For enhanced security, compliance, and control over resources

questions.question: What is a Hybrid Cloud?
options: A cloud model that combines public cloud and private cloud environments
A cloud that supports only AI workloads

questions.question: Which scenario best demonstrates a Hybrid Cloud approach?
options: Running all workloads in a public cloud
Keeping sensitive data in a private cloud while hosting customer-facing applications in a public cloud
Storing files on a personal laptop

questions.question: What is a key advantage of Public Cloud?
options: High scalability and lower upfront infrastructure costs

questions.question: What is a major benefit of Hybrid Cloud?
options: Flexibility to place workloads in the most appropriate environment

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes Public, Private, and Hybrid Cloud models?
options: Public Cloud is shared infrastructure, Private Cloud is dedicated infrastructure, and Hybrid Cloud combines both approaches
Private Cloud is less secure than Public Cloud
Hybrid Cloud replaces Public Cloud entirely

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of AWS Global Infrastructure?
options: To provide highly available, scalable, and low-latency cloud services worldwide

questions.question: What is an AWS Region?
options: A geographic area containing multiple Availability Zones

questions.question: Why does AWS have multiple Regions around the world?
options: To provide redundancy, compliance options, and lower latency for customers

questions.question: What is an Availability Zone (AZ)?
options: A collection of one or more physically separate data centers within a Region

questions.question: Why are Availability Zones physically separated?
options: To reduce the impact of failures such as power outages or natural disasters

questions.question: Which AWS infrastructure component is primarily used to deliver content with low latency to end users?
What is the relationship between Regions and Availability Zones?
options: A Region contains multiple Availability Zones
An Availability Zone contains multiple Regions

questions.question: A company wants high availability for its application. Which AWS design practice should it follow?
options: Deploy resources across multiple Availability Zones
Store all resources on one server

questions.question: Which AWS Global Infrastructure component helps reduce latency by serving content closer to users?
Which statement best summarizes AWS Global Infrastructure?
options: AWS Global Infrastructure consists of Regions, Availability Zones, and Edge Locations that provide scalability, fault tolerance, and low-latency services worldwide
AWS operates from a single global data center
Regions and Availability Zones are the same thing
Edge Locations are used only for storage

questions.question: What is an AWS Region?
options: A virtual machine running in AWS
A geographic area that contains multiple Availability Zones

questions.question: What is an Availability Zone (AZ)?
options: One or more isolated data centers within an AWS Region

questions.question: How many Availability Zones does an AWS Region typically contain?
Why does AWS use multiple Availability Zones within a Region?
options: To improve high availability and fault tolerance

questions.question: Which statement best describes the relationship between Regions and Availability Zones?
Why are Availability Zones physically separated from each other?
options: To reduce the risk of a single failure affecting all resources

questions.question: A company deploys its application across two Availability Zones. What benefit does this provide?
Which AWS infrastructure component should you choose when you want to deploy resources closer to users in a specific geographic location?
What is a common reason for selecting a specific AWS Region?
options: Lower latency, compliance requirements, or proximity to customers

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes Regions and Availability Zones?
options: Regions are geographic locations that contain multiple isolated Availability Zones designed for high availability and fault tolerance
Regions and Availability Zones are the same thing
Availability Zones are used only for storage

questions.question: What is the AWS Shared Responsibility Model?
options: A pricing model used by AWS
A framework that defines security responsibilities shared between AWS and customers

questions.question: What does AWS mean by "Security of the Cloud"?
options: Protecting the AWS infrastructure that runs cloud services

questions.question: What does the customer typically manage under "Security in the Cloud"?
options: User access, data protection, and application security

questions.question: Which of the following is AWS's responsibility?
options: Managing operating system patches on customer-managed EC2 instances

questions.question: Which of the following is typically the customer's responsibility when using Amazon EC2?
options: Managing the guest operating system and its updates

questions.question: Why is the Shared Responsibility Model important?
options: It clarifies security ownership and helps prevent security gaps

questions.question: In Amazon S3, who is responsible for configuring bucket permissions?
Which statement best describes responsibility for data encryption?
options: AWS always encrypts and manages customer data automatically
Customers are generally responsible for deciding how their data is encrypted and protected
Encryption is not required in cloud environments

questions.question: How does the Shared Responsibility Model change with managed services such as AWS RDS?
options: AWS assumes more operational responsibilities compared to EC2

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes the AWS Shared Responsibility Model?
options: AWS secures the cloud infrastructure, while customers secure their data, applications, and configurations within the cloud

questions.question: What does IAM stand for in AWS?
What is the primary purpose of AWS IAM?
options: To manage authentication and authorization for AWS resources

questions.question: What is an IAM User?
options: A digital identity created for an individual person or application requiring AWS access

questions.question: Why are IAM Groups used?
options: To organize IAM users and assign permissions collectively

questions.question: A company has 20 developers who need identical AWS permissions. What is the recommended approach?
options: Create separate policies for each user
Add all developers to an IAM Group with the required permissions

questions.question: What is an IAM Role?
options: An AWS identity that provides temporary permissions and can be assumed by users, applications, or services

questions.question: Which AWS service commonly uses IAM Roles to access other AWS resources securely?
Why are IAM Roles generally preferred over hard-coded access keys in applications?
options: They provide temporary credentials and improve security

questions.question: Which AWS security principle recommends granting only the permissions necessary to perform a task?
Which statement best summarizes IAM Users, Groups, and Roles?
options: Users represent identities, Groups simplify permission management, and Roles provide temporary access to AWS resources
Users, Groups, and Roles perform the same function

questions.question: What is an IAM Policy in AWS?
options: A JSON document that defines permissions for AWS resources and actions

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of an IAM Policy?
options: To define permissions and control access to AWS services and resources

questions.question: Which format is used to write AWS IAM Policies?
What are the main elements of an IAM Policy statement?
What does the "Effect" field in an IAM Policy specify?
options: Whether an action is Allowed or Denied

questions.question: What is a Permission Boundary in AWS IAM?
options: A maximum limit that defines the highest permissions an IAM user or role can receive

questions.question: Why are Permission Boundaries useful in large organizations?
options: They help enforce security controls and prevent excessive permissions

questions.question: If an IAM Role has a policy allowing S3 access but a Permission Boundary does not allow S3 actions, what happens?
options: S3 access is denied because Permission Boundaries limit maximum permissions

questions.question: Which statement best describes the relationship between IAM Policies and Permission Boundaries?
options: Policies grant permissions, while Permission Boundaries define the maximum permissions that can be granted
Permission Boundaries automatically grant administrator access

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes IAM Policies and Permission Boundaries?
options: IAM Policies define access permissions, while Permission Boundaries restrict the maximum permissions users and roles can obtain
Both are used only for billing purposes
Policies and Boundaries are unrelated to security

questions.question: What does MFA stand for in AWS security?
Why is MFA important for AWS accounts?
options: It helps protect accounts even if passwords are compromised

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a second authentication factor?
options: One-Time Password (OTP) generated by an authenticator app

questions.question: What is the recommended security practice for the AWS root account?
options: Enable MFA and avoid routine use of the root account
Use the root account for all daily activities

questions.question: What is a credential in AWS?
options: Information used to authenticate an identity, such as usernames, passwords, or access keys

questions.question: Why should access keys be protected carefully?
options: They grant programmatic access to AWS resources and can be misused if exposed

questions.question: What is a recommended alternative to hard-coded access keys in AWS applications?
Which security practice helps reduce the risk of credential compromise?
options: Regular credential rotation and strong password policies

questions.question: A developer accidentally publishes AWS access keys in a public repository. What should be done first?
options: Immediately disable or rotate the exposed credentials and investigate usage

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes MFA and Credential Security?
options: MFA adds additional verification layers, while credential security focuses on protecting passwords, access keys, and authentication mechanisms
Credential security is only AWS's responsibility
MFA is only required for administrators

questions.question: What is AWS Organizations?
options: A storage service for AWS accounts
A service that helps centrally manage and govern multiple AWS accounts

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of AWS Organizations?
What is the top-level container in AWS Organizations called?
What is an Organizational Unit (OU)?
options: A logical grouping of AWS accounts within an organization

questions.question: Why do organizations create multiple AWS accounts?
options: To improve governance, security isolation, and billing management

questions.question: What is consolidated billing in AWS Organizations?
options: A feature that combines billing from multiple AWS accounts into a single bill

questions.question: What is a Service Control Policy (SCP)?
options: A policy that manages EC2 instances
A governance policy that sets permission boundaries across AWS accounts in an organization

questions.question: Which statement best describes the relationship between SCPs and IAM Policies?
options: SCPs define maximum permissions, while IAM Policies grant actual permissions

questions.question: A company wants separate AWS accounts for Development, Testing, and Production environments. Which AWS Organizations feature helps structure this setup?
Which statement best summarizes AWS Organizations?
options: AWS Organizations provides centralized account management, governance, consolidated billing, and security controls across multiple AWS accounts
AWS Organizations is used only for storage management
AWS Organizations is a networking service

questions.question: What does EC2 stand for in AWS?
What is the primary purpose of Amazon EC2?
options: Running virtual machines and applications in the cloud

questions.question: What is an EC2 Instance?
options: A virtual server running in AWS

questions.question: What is an Amazon Machine Image (AMI)?
options: A template used to launch EC2 instances with predefined configurations

questions.question: Which component is typically included in an AMI?
Why are different EC2 instance types available?
options: To support different workload requirements such as compute, memory, storage, and networking needs

questions.question: Which EC2 instance family is commonly optimized for general-purpose workloads?
Which EC2 instance family is designed for memory-intensive applications such as databases?
A company needs high-performance computing for scientific simulations. Which EC2 instance family would generally be most appropriate?
Which statement best summarizes EC2 Instance Types and AMIs?
options: AMIs provide the template for launching EC2 instances, while instance types define the hardware resources available to the virtual server
AMIs and instance types are identical concepts

questions.question: What does EBS stand for in AWS?
What is the primary purpose of an EBS Volume?
options: To provide persistent storage for EC2 instances

questions.question: What happens to data stored on an EBS volume when an EC2 instance is stopped?
options: The data remains stored and can be accessed when the instance starts again
The data is moved to Amazon S3

questions.question: Which storage type best describes Amazon EBS?
Can an EBS volume be detached from one EC2 instance and attached to another?
options: Yes, in many cases within the same Availability Zone
Only after converting it to S3

questions.question: What is an EBS Snapshot?
options: A copy of an EC2 instance
A backup of an EBS volume stored in AWS-managed storage

questions.question: Why are EBS Snapshots useful?
options: They allow backup, disaster recovery, and volume restoration

questions.question: Where are EBS Snapshots stored?
options: In AWS-managed storage (internally backed by Amazon S3)

questions.question: A company wants to create a backup before updating a production server. What is the recommended action?
options: Create an EBS Snapshot of the attached volume

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes EBS Volumes and Snapshots?
options: EBS Volumes provide persistent block storage for EC2 instances, while Snapshots create point-in-time backups of those volumes
EBS Volumes and Snapshots are identical
EBS is used only for networking

questions.question: What is an AWS Placement Group?
options: A storage service for EC2 instances
A logical grouping of EC2 instances that influences how they are placed within AWS infrastructure

questions.question: Why are Placement Groups used?
options: To optimize performance, networking, or availability depending on workload requirements

questions.question: How many main types of Placement Groups are available in AWS?
Which Placement Group strategy is designed to provide the lowest network latency and highest throughput between EC2 instances?
Which workload is best suited for a Cluster Placement Group?
What is the primary goal of a Spread Placement Group?
options: Place instances across distinct hardware to reduce the risk of simultaneous failure

questions.question: Which Placement Group strategy is commonly used for critical applications requiring high availability?
What is a Partition Placement Group?
options: A strategy that divides instances into logical partitions to improve fault isolation at scale

questions.question: Which workload is commonly associated with Partition Placement Groups?
options: Large distributed systems such as Hadoop or Kafka clusters

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes AWS Placement Groups?
options: Placement Groups control how EC2 instances are physically deployed to optimize performance, latency, and fault tolerance
Placement Groups are used only for storage management

questions.question: What is an AWS Launch Template?
options: A backup service for EC2 instances
A reusable configuration template used to launch EC2 instances

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a Launch Template?
options: To automate and standardize EC2 instance deployments

questions.question: Which of the following can be defined within a Launch Template?
options: AMI, instance type, security groups, and storage configuration

questions.question: Why are Launch Templates useful in large-scale environments?
options: They ensure consistent configuration across multiple EC2 instances

questions.question: Which AWS service commonly integrates with Launch Templates for automatic scaling?
What advantage do Launch Template versions provide?
options: The ability to track and manage configuration changes over time

questions.question: A company wants every new EC2 instance to use the same AMI, security group, and instance type. What AWS feature should be used?
Which configuration is NOT typically stored in a Launch Template?
How do Launch Templates improve operational efficiency?
options: By reducing repetitive manual configuration tasks

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes AWS Launch Templates?
options: Launch Templates provide reusable EC2 deployment configurations that support automation, consistency, and scalability
Launch Templates are used only for storage management

questions.question: What does S3 stand for in AWS?
What type of storage does Amazon S3 provide?
What is an S3 Bucket?
options: A container used to store objects in Amazon S3

questions.question: What is an S3 Object?
options: A file stored in an S3 bucket along with its metadata

questions.question: Which feature makes Amazon S3 highly durable?
options: Data is redundantly stored across multiple devices within an AWS Region
Files are stored on a single server
Storage is limited to one Availability Zone
Objects are automatically deleted after upload

questions.question: What is an S3 Storage Class?
options: A security policy for S3 buckets
A category that defines the cost, availability, and access characteristics of stored data

questions.question: Which S3 Storage Class is designed for frequently accessed data?
Which S3 Storage Class is best suited for long-term archival data that is rarely accessed?
What is the purpose of S3 Intelligent-Tiering?
options: Automatically move data between access tiers based on usage patterns

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes Amazon S3 and Storage Classes?
options: Amazon S3 is an object storage service, and Storage Classes allow organizations to balance cost and access requirements for different types of data
S3 is a block storage service only
Storage Classes are used only for security purposes

questions.question: What does EBS stand for in AWS?
What does EFS stand for in AWS?
What type of storage does Amazon EBS provide?
What type of storage does Amazon EFS provide?
Which AWS storage service is typically attached to a single EC2 instance?
Which AWS storage service can be accessed by multiple EC2 instances simultaneously?
Which storage solution would be most suitable for a shared web application where multiple servers need access to the same files?
Which storage service is commonly used as the primary disk for an EC2 instance?
What is a major advantage of Amazon EFS?
options: Shared file access with automatic scaling
Lower latency than all storage services

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes EBS and EFS?
options: EBS provides block storage for individual EC2 instances, while EFS provides scalable shared file storage for multiple EC2 instances
EBS and EFS are identical services
EFS is object storage and EBS is file storage
Both are used only for backups

questions.question: What is S3 Versioning?
options: A feature that automatically increases storage size
A feature that keeps multiple versions of an object in an S3 bucket

questions.question: Why is S3 Versioning useful?
options: It protects against accidental deletion and overwriting of objects
It creates new AWS accounts automatically

questions.question: What happens when an object is updated in a version-enabled S3 bucket?
options: The old version is deleted permanently
A new version is created while previous versions are retained

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of an S3 Lifecycle Policy?
options: To automate storage management and optimize costs

questions.question: Which action can a Lifecycle Policy perform?
options: Move objects to a lower-cost storage class after a specific period

questions.question: A company stores logs that are rarely accessed after 90 days. Which Lifecycle Policy action would be most appropriate?
options: Transition objects to a lower-cost storage class such as Glacier

questions.question: What is a common use case for S3 Versioning?
options: Recovering accidentally deleted or modified files

questions.question: Can Lifecycle Policies be applied to previous object versions?
options: Yes, Lifecycle Policies can manage current and non-current object versions
No, only current versions can be managed
Only IAM administrators can manage versions manually
Versioning and Lifecycle Policies cannot work together

questions.question: Which combination provides both data protection and cost optimization in Amazon S3?
Which statement best summarizes Lifecycle Policies and Versioning?
options: Versioning protects object history, while Lifecycle Policies automate storage transitions and deletion to optimize costs
Both features perform the same function
Versioning is only used for networking

questions.question: What is encryption?
options: The process of converting readable data into an unreadable format to protect it from unauthorized access

questions.question: What is Encryption at Rest?
options: Encrypting data while it is stored on disks, databases, or storage services
Encrypting data while it travels across a network

questions.question: Which AWS service commonly supports Encryption at Rest?
What is Encryption in Transit?
options: Encrypting data while it is being transferred between systems or over a network

questions.question: Which protocol is commonly used to provide Encryption in Transit for web applications?
Why is Encryption at Rest important?
options: It protects stored data from unauthorized access if storage media is compromised
It eliminates the need for backups

questions.question: Why is Encryption in Transit important?
options: It protects data from interception or tampering during transmission

questions.question: Which AWS service is commonly used to manage encryption keys?
A company stores customer records in Amazon S3 and accesses them through HTTPS. Which types of encryption are being used?
options: Both Encryption at Rest and Encryption in Transit

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes Encryption at Rest and Encryption in Transit?
options: Encryption at Rest protects stored data, while Encryption in Transit protects data as it moves between systems
Encryption is only required during transmission
Encryption is only required for stored data

questions.question: What does VPC stand for in AWS?
What is the primary purpose of an AWS VPC?
options: To provide an isolated networking environment for AWS resources

questions.question: What is a subnet in AWS?
options: A smaller network segment within a VPC

questions.question: Why are subnets used inside a VPC?
options: To organize resources and control network access

questions.question: What is a Public Subnet?
options: A subnet that has a route to the internet through an Internet Gateway
A subnet used only for storage
A subnet used only for databases

questions.question: What is a Private Subnet?
options: A subnet connected directly to the internet
A subnet without direct internet access
A subnet that contains only databases
A subnet used only by AWS services

questions.question: What is a Route Table?
options: A set of routing rules that determine where network traffic is directed

questions.question: What is the role of an Internet Gateway (IGW) in a VPC?
options: To provide internet connectivity for resources in public subnets

questions.question: A company hosts a web server in a public subnet and a database in a private subnet. What is the main advantage of this architecture?
options: Improved security by preventing direct internet access to the database

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes VPCs, Subnets, and Route Tables?
options: A VPC provides an isolated network, subnets divide the network into segments, and route tables control traffic flow between resources
They are all used only for storage management

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of an Internet Gateway (IGW) in AWS?
options: To provide internet connectivity between a VPC and the public internet

questions.question: Which AWS networking component must be attached to a VPC to enable internet access?
Which type of subnet typically uses an Internet Gateway?
What is a NAT Gateway?
options: A service that allows resources in private subnets to access the internet without accepting inbound internet connections

questions.question: Why would an EC2 instance in a private subnet need a NAT Gateway?
options: To download software updates or access external services without being publicly accessible

questions.question: Where should a NAT Gateway be deployed?
Which statement correctly describes inbound internet traffic to resources behind a NAT Gateway?
options: Direct inbound internet connections are not allowed
All inbound traffic is automatically allowed
NAT Gateway acts as a load balancer

questions.question: What is a key security benefit of using private subnets with a NAT Gateway?
options: Resources can access the internet without being directly exposed to external users
It removes the need for firewalls

questions.question: A company places web servers in a public subnet and database servers in a private subnet. Which component allows the database servers to download operating system updates?
Which statement best summarizes Internet Gateway and NAT Gateway?
options: Internet Gateways provide direct internet access for public resources, while NAT Gateways allow private resources to access the internet securely without accepting inbound connections
Both services perform exactly the same function
NAT Gateways provide inbound internet access to private resources

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a Security Group in AWS?
At which level does a Security Group operate?
What is a Network Access Control List (NACL)?
options: A subnet-level firewall that controls inbound and outbound traffic

questions.question: At which level does a NACL operate?
Which of the following is stateful?
Which of the following is stateless?
Which AWS security feature supports only Allow rules?
Which AWS security feature supports both Allow and Deny rules?
A company wants to block a specific IP address from accessing an entire subnet. Which AWS feature is best suited for this requirement?
Which statement best summarizes Security Groups and NACLs?
options: Security Groups are stateful instance-level firewalls, while NACLs are stateless subnet-level firewalls that support both allow and deny rules
Security Groups operate at subnet level and NACLs operate at instance level

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a multi-tier architecture in AWS?
options: To separate application components for security, scalability, and manageability

questions.question: What is typically placed in the Public Tier of an AWS architecture?
options: Internet-facing resources such as Web Servers and Load Balancers

questions.question: What is typically placed in the Private Tier?
Why are databases commonly deployed in private subnets?
options: To improve security by preventing direct internet access

questions.question: Which AWS component allows internet traffic to reach resources in the Public Tier?
How do resources in a Private Tier typically access the internet for updates and patches?
What is a common three-tier architecture design?
options: Web Tier, Application Tier, Database Tier
Storage Tier, IAM Tier, DNS Tier
Region Tier, AZ Tier, Subnet Tier
Route Tier, Firewall Tier, Backup Tier

questions.question: What is the primary security benefit of separating public and private tiers?
options: Reduced attack surface and better access control

questions.question: A web application receives requests from users, processes them through application servers, and stores data in a database. Which component should be in the Private Tier?
Which statement best summarizes Public & Private Tier Design?
options: Public tiers host internet-facing resources, while private tiers host internal systems that require additional security and controlled access
Public and private tiers serve the same purpose
Private tiers require direct internet access
Public tiers are more secure than private tiers

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of an Application Load Balancer (ALB)?
options: To distribute incoming application traffic across multiple targets

questions.question: At which layer of the OSI model does an Application Load Balancer operate?
Which protocols are commonly supported by an Application Load Balancer?
What is a Target Group in an ALB?
options: A collection of resources that receive traffic from the load balancer

questions.question: What is a major benefit of using an ALB?
options: High availability through traffic distribution across multiple targets

questions.question: What is Path-Based Routing in an ALB?
options: Routing requests based on URL paths such as /api or /images
Routing based on Availability Zones only

questions.question: What is Host-Based Routing in an ALB?
options: Routing traffic based on domain names or host headers
Routing based on EC2 instance size
Routing based on EBS volume size

questions.question: Why are health checks important in an ALB?
options: They identify unhealthy targets and stop routing traffic to them

questions.question: A company hosts multiple web applications behind a single ALB. Which ALB feature allows traffic to be routed to the correct application?
Which statement best summarizes an Application Load Balancer?
options: An ALB distributes HTTP/HTTPS traffic across multiple targets and supports advanced routing features for scalable, highly available applications
An ALB is a storage service
An ALB replaces Auto Scaling Groups

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a Network Load Balancer (NLB)?
options: To distribute network traffic with ultra-high performance and low latency

questions.question: At which layer of the OSI model does a Network Load Balancer operate?
Which protocols are commonly supported by a Network Load Balancer?
What is a key advantage of an NLB compared to an Application Load Balancer?
options: Lower latency and higher performance for network traffic

questions.question: Which type of workload is best suited for a Network Load Balancer?
options: High-performance TCP applications and gaming services

questions.question: Which AWS load balancer preserves the client's source IP address by default?
Why are health checks important in a Network Load Balancer?
options: They ensure traffic is routed only to healthy targets

questions.question: A company runs a real-time gaming application that requires very low latency. Which AWS load balancer is the best choice?
Which routing capability is supported by an NLB?
Which statement best summarizes a Network Load Balancer?
options: An NLB operates at Layer 4, providing ultra-high performance, low latency, and scalable traffic distribution for TCP, UDP, and TLS workloads
An NLB is used only for storage services

questions.question: What is a Target Group in AWS Elastic Load Balancing?
options: A collection of resources that receive traffic from a load balancer

questions.question: Which AWS resources can commonly be registered as targets?
What is the primary purpose of a Target Group?
options: To organize and route traffic to backend resources

questions.question: What is a Health Check in AWS Load Balancing?
options: A mechanism used to determine whether a target is available and functioning correctly

questions.question: Why are Health Checks important?
options: They improve application availability by detecting unhealthy targets

questions.question: What happens when a target repeatedly fails health checks?
options: It is marked unhealthy and removed from traffic routing

questions.question: Which protocol is commonly used for Application Load Balancer health checks?
A load balancer has three EC2 instances in a Target Group. One instance becomes unavailable. What happens?
options: Traffic continues to be sent to all instances equally
The unhealthy instance is removed from rotation, and traffic is sent to healthy instances only

questions.question: Which AWS feature works closely with Target Groups to support high availability?
Which statement best summarizes Target Groups and Health Checks?
options: Target Groups define traffic destinations, while Health Checks ensure traffic is routed only to healthy resources
Target Groups are used only for storage services

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of an Auto Scaling Group (ASG)?
options: To automatically adjust the number of EC2 instances based on demand

questions.question: What problem does Auto Scaling solve?
Which AWS component is typically used by an Auto Scaling Group to launch new EC2 instances?
What is the desired capacity in an Auto Scaling Group?
options: The number of EC2 instances the group attempts to maintain
The storage size of EBS volumes

questions.question: What is a Scaling Policy?
options: A set of rules that determines when Auto Scaling should add or remove instances

questions.question: Which metric is commonly used to trigger Auto Scaling?
What is Scale-Out?
options: Adding more EC2 instances when demand increases
Removing EC2 instances when demand decreases

questions.question: What is Scale-In?
options: Removing unnecessary EC2 instances when demand decreases

questions.question: A web application experiences a sudden traffic spike. Which AWS service helps automatically launch additional servers?
Which statement best summarizes Auto Scaling Groups and Policies?
options: Auto Scaling Groups maintain the desired number of EC2 instances, while Scaling Policies automatically adjust capacity based on demand
Auto Scaling Groups are used only for storage services
Auto Scaling is used only for databases

questions.question: What does RDS stand for in AWS?
What is the primary benefit of Amazon RDS?
options: AWS manages database infrastructure tasks such as backups, patching, and maintenance

questions.question: Which type of database does Amazon RDS provide?
What does Multi-AZ mean in Amazon RDS?
options: Database deployment across multiple Availability Zones for high availability

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a Multi-AZ deployment?
What happens if the primary database instance fails in a Multi-AZ deployment?
options: Traffic automatically fails over to the standby instance
A new AWS Region is created
Users lose all database access permanently

questions.question: Does a Multi-AZ standby instance serve application read traffic?
options: No, it is primarily used for failover purposes

questions.question: Which AWS feature automatically handles database backups in Amazon RDS?
A company runs a production database that requires minimal downtime. Which Amazon RDS feature should be enabled?
Which statement best summarizes Amazon RDS and Multi-AZ?
options: Amazon RDS is a managed relational database service, and Multi-AZ deployments provide high availability through automatic failover to a standby database
Multi-AZ is used only for backups

questions.question: What is Amazon DynamoDB?
options: A fully managed NoSQL database service provided by AWS

questions.question: What type of database is DynamoDB?
What is the primary data structure in DynamoDB?
What is an Item in DynamoDB?
options: A single record stored within a table

questions.question: What is an Attribute in DynamoDB?
options: A column-like piece of data associated with an item

questions.question: Why is the Primary Key important in DynamoDB?
options: It uniquely identifies items within a table

questions.question: Which feature makes DynamoDB highly scalable?
Which use case is well-suited for DynamoDB?
options: Real-time applications requiring low-latency access at scale
Traditional relational reporting with complex joins

questions.question: What is a key advantage of DynamoDB compared to self-managed databases?
options: AWS handles infrastructure, scaling, availability, and maintenance
It requires operating system patching by customers
It can only store structured SQL data

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes DynamoDB?
options: DynamoDB is a fully managed NoSQL database service that provides high performance, automatic scaling, and low-latency access to data
DynamoDB is a relational database service only
DynamoDB is used only for backups

questions.question: What is Amazon ElastiCache?
options: A managed caching service that improves application performance

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of ElastiCache?
options: To reduce application latency and improve performance

questions.question: Which type of storage does ElastiCache primarily use?
Which caching engines are supported by Amazon ElastiCache?
Why is caching commonly used in modern applications?
options: To reduce repeated database queries and improve response times

questions.question: What is a Cache Hit?
options: When requested data is found in the cache
When a security group blocks traffic

questions.question: What is a Cache Miss?
options: When data is unavailable in the cache and must be retrieved from the original data source

questions.question: Which application scenario is most likely to benefit from ElastiCache?
options: Frequently accessed product catalog data in an e-commerce application

questions.question: How does ElastiCache improve database performance?
options: By serving frequently requested data from memory and reducing database workload

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes Amazon ElastiCache?
options: ElastiCache is a managed in-memory caching service that improves application speed, reduces latency, and decreases database load
ElastiCache is a relational database service
ElastiCache is used only for backups

questions.question: What is Amazon Aurora?
options: A fully managed relational database engine built for the cloud

questions.question: Amazon Aurora is compatible with which popular database engines?
Which AWS service family does Aurora belong to?
What is a key advantage of Aurora compared to traditional self-managed databases?
options: AWS handles infrastructure, backups, patching, and high availability
Aurora requires manual scaling for every operation

questions.question: How does Aurora improve availability?
options: By replicating data across multiple Availability Zones automatically
By storing data on a single server

questions.question: What is an Aurora Replica?
options: A read-only database instance used to scale read workloads

questions.question: Which type of workload benefits most from Aurora Replicas?
What happens if the primary Aurora instance becomes unavailable?
options: Aurora can automatically fail over to a replica
Users must manually recreate the database

questions.question: Why do organizations choose Aurora for production applications?
options: High performance, scalability, availability, and managed operations

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes Amazon Aurora?
options: Aurora is a fully managed relational database service that offers high performance, automatic replication, scalability, and fault tolerance
Aurora is a NoSQL database service
Aurora is a storage service only

questions.question: What is Amazon Route 53?
options: A scalable DNS (Domain Name System) and traffic routing service

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a Route 53 Routing Policy?
options: To determine how DNS queries are answered and where traffic is directed

questions.question: Which Route 53 routing policy routes traffic to a single resource?
Which routing policy distributes traffic across multiple resources based on assigned percentages?
A company wants to send 80% of traffic to Server A and 20% to Server B during testing. Which routing policy should be used?
Which Route 53 routing policy directs users to the AWS Region with the lowest network latency?
Which routing policy sends users to resources based on their geographic location?
What is the purpose of Failover Routing?
options: To provide disaster recovery by directing traffic to a backup resource when the primary resource becomes unavailable

questions.question: Which Route 53 routing policy returns multiple healthy records in response to a DNS query?
Which statement best summarizes Route 53 Routing Policies?
options: Route 53 Routing Policies determine how DNS traffic is directed based on requirements such as performance, availability, testing, and geographic location
Route 53 is used only for storage services
Routing Policies replace Load Balancers entirely

questions.question: What is Amazon CloudFront?
options: A Content Delivery Network (CDN) service that delivers content with low latency and high performance

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a CDN?
options: To deliver content faster by serving it from locations closer to users

questions.question: Which AWS service provides CDN functionality?
What are Edge Locations in CloudFront?
options: Data centers that cache content closer to users worldwide

questions.question: Which type of content is commonly delivered through CloudFront?
options: Images, videos, websites, APIs, and static files

questions.question: What is an Origin in CloudFront?
options: The source location from which CloudFront retrieves content

questions.question: What happens when a user requests content that is already cached at an Edge Location?
options: CloudFront retrieves it from the origin again
CloudFront serves it directly from the cache, reducing latency
A new Edge Location is created

questions.question: What is a Cache Miss in CloudFront?
options: When requested content is not available at the Edge Location and must be fetched from the origin

questions.question: How does CloudFront improve application performance?
options: By reducing latency and offloading traffic from origin servers
By increasing EC2 instance size automatically

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes CloudFront CDN Delivery?
options: CloudFront is a global CDN service that caches content at Edge Locations to deliver applications, websites, and media with low latency and high performance

questions.question: What is AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)?
options: A service that provisions, manages, and deploys SSL/TLS certificates for AWS resources

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of an SSL/TLS certificate?
options: To encrypt data transmitted between clients and servers

questions.question: Which protocol commonly uses SSL/TLS certificates?
What is a major benefit of using AWS Certificate Manager?
Which AWS service commonly integrates with ACM to provide HTTPS support?
Which AWS CDN service commonly uses ACM certificates for secure content delivery?
Why is certificate renewal important?
options: Expired certificates can cause security warnings and service disruptions

questions.question: What does ACM automate for AWS-managed certificates?
A company wants to secure its website with HTTPS while minimizing operational overhead. Which AWS service should it use?
Which statement best summarizes AWS Certificate Manager?
options: ACM simplifies SSL/TLS certificate provisioning, deployment, and automatic renewal for AWS applications and services
ACM is used only for backups

questions.question: What is AWS Lambda?
options: A serverless compute service that runs code without managing servers

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of AWS Lambda?
What does "serverless" mean in AWS Lambda?
options: Developers do not need to manage the underlying servers
Lambda runs only on local machines

questions.question: What triggers an AWS Lambda function?
options: Events from AWS services, applications, or external systems

questions.question: Which AWS service commonly triggers Lambda when a file is uploaded?
What is an execution model in AWS Lambda?
options: The way Lambda functions are invoked and executed in response to events

questions.question: Which execution model waits for the function to complete before returning a response?
Which execution model allows the caller to continue immediately while Lambda processes the request in the background?
A web application sends a request to Lambda and requires an immediate response. Which execution model is most appropriate?
Which statement best summarizes AWS Lambda and its execution models?
options: AWS Lambda is a serverless compute service that executes code in response to events using synchronous or asynchronous invocation models
Lambda is used only for backups

questions.question: What is Amazon API Gateway?
options: A fully managed service for creating, publishing, securing, and monitoring APIs

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of API Gateway?
options: To provide a secure entry point for applications and backend services

questions.question: What does REST stand for in REST API?
Which HTTP method is commonly used to retrieve data in a REST API?
Which AWS service is most commonly integrated with API Gateway in serverless architectures?
What is an API Endpoint?
options: A URL through which clients access an API

questions.question: Why is API Gateway useful for security?
options: It supports authentication, authorization, throttling, and encryption
It removes the need for HTTPS

questions.question: What is throttling in API Gateway?
options: Limiting the number of API requests to protect backend systems

questions.question: A mobile application needs to call a serverless backend built with Lambda. Which AWS service should expose the API endpoint?
Which statement best summarizes Amazon API Gateway REST APIs?
options: API Gateway enables organizations to create, secure, manage, and scale REST APIs while integrating with AWS services such as Lambda
API Gateway is a storage service
API Gateway is used only for monitoring

questions.question: What is Amazon SNS?
options: A fully managed publish/subscribe messaging service

questions.question: What is Amazon SQS?
What communication model does SNS primarily use?
What communication model does SQS primarily use?
What is a key benefit of Amazon SNS?
options: One message can be delivered to multiple subscribers simultaneously

questions.question: What is a key benefit of Amazon SQS?
options: Decoupling application components for improved reliability and scalability

questions.question: What happens to a message in SQS after it is sent?
options: It remains in the queue until a consumer processes it

questions.question: A company wants to send one notification to multiple systems, including email, Lambda, and SQS queues. Which AWS service is most appropriate?
Which AWS service is commonly used to buffer workloads and prevent application overload?
Which statement best summarizes SNS and SQS?
options: SNS provides publish/subscribe messaging for notifications, while SQS provides message queues for asynchronous communication between application components
SNS and SQS perform exactly the same function
SNS is used only for databases

questions.question: What is Amazon EventBridge?
options: A serverless event bus service that routes events between applications and AWS services

questions.question: What is an event in EventBridge?
options: A notification that something has happened in a system or application

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of EventBridge?
options: To route events from sources to targets based on defined rules

questions.question: What is an Event Source in EventBridge?
options: The service or application that generates events

questions.question: What is a Rule in EventBridge?
options: A condition that determines which events should be routed to which targets

questions.question: What is a Target in EventBridge?
options: The destination service that receives an event after a rule matches it

questions.question: Which AWS service is commonly triggered by EventBridge for serverless automation?
A company wants to automatically trigger a Lambda function whenever a file is uploaded to Amazon S3. Which AWS service can route this event?
What is a major benefit of EventBridge?
Which statement best summarizes Amazon EventBridge?
options: EventBridge is a serverless event bus that routes events from sources to targets using rules, enabling scalable event-driven architectures
EventBridge is used only for monitoring

questions.question: What is Amazon CloudWatch?
options: A monitoring and observability service for AWS resources and applications

questions.question: What is a CloudWatch Metric?
options: A measurable data point that represents the performance or status of a resource

questions.question: Which CloudWatch metric is commonly monitored for Amazon EC2 instances?
What are CloudWatch Logs?
options: A service that collects, stores, and analyzes log data from AWS resources and applications

questions.question: Why are logs important in cloud environments?
options: They provide visibility into application behavior and system events

questions.question: What is a CloudWatch Alarm?
options: A feature that monitors metrics and performs actions when thresholds are reached

questions.question: What action can a CloudWatch Alarm perform?
A company wants to receive an alert when EC2 CPU utilization exceeds 80%. Which CloudWatch feature should be used?
How can CloudWatch contribute to cost optimization?
options: By identifying underutilized resources and monitoring usage trends

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes CloudWatch Metrics, Logs, and Alarms?
options: CloudWatch Metrics track performance data, Logs capture system activity, and Alarms automate notifications and responses based on defined thresholds
All three components perform the same function
CloudWatch is only used for security monitoring

questions.question: What is AWS CloudTrail?
options: A service that records and tracks AWS account activity and API calls

questions.question: What type of information does CloudTrail record?
options: API calls and account activity across AWS services

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of CloudTrail?
options: Auditing, governance, security monitoring, and compliance

questions.question: Which question can CloudTrail help answer?
options: When was an EC2 instance terminated?

questions.question: What is an Event in CloudTrail?
options: A record of an action performed within an AWS account

questions.question: Which information is commonly included in a CloudTrail event?
options: User identity, timestamp, source IP, and action performed

questions.question: How does CloudTrail support security investigations?
options: By providing a historical record of AWS account activity

questions.question: A company wants to know who changed an IAM role yesterday. Which AWS service should be used?
What is the difference between CloudTrail and CloudWatch?
options: CloudTrail tracks account activity and API calls, while CloudWatch focuses on monitoring metrics, logs, and operational performance

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes AWS CloudTrail?
options: CloudTrail records AWS account activity and API calls, providing auditing, compliance, governance, and security visibility across AWS environments
CloudTrail is used only for monitoring CPU utilization

questions.question: What is AWS Config?
options: A service that records, tracks, and evaluates AWS resource configurations over time

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of AWS Config?
options: Resource configuration tracking and compliance monitoring

questions.question: What type of information does AWS Config record?
What is a Configuration Item in AWS Config?
options: A snapshot of an AWS resource's configuration at a specific point in time

questions.question: What is an AWS Config Rule?
options: A compliance rule used to evaluate resource configurations against desired settings

questions.question: A company requires all S3 buckets to have encryption enabled. Which AWS Config feature can verify this requirement?
What does a "Non-Compliant" result indicate in AWS Config?
options: A resource does not meet the requirements defined by a Config Rule

questions.question: How does AWS Config support security and governance?
options: By continuously evaluating resources against compliance standards and organizational policies

questions.question: What is the relationship between AWS Config and compliance auditing?
options: AWS Config provides historical configuration data and compliance status for audit purposes

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes AWS Config?
options: AWS Config tracks resource configurations, records configuration history, and evaluates compliance using configurable rules
AWS Config is a database service
AWS Config is used only for monitoring CPU usage

questions.question: What is AWS CloudFormation?
options: An Infrastructure as Code (IaC) service used to provision and manage AWS resources using templates

questions.question: What is Infrastructure as Code (IaC)?
options: Managing infrastructure through manual console operations only
Defining and provisioning infrastructure using code and configuration files

questions.question: What is a CloudFormation Template?
options: A file that describes AWS resources and their configurations

questions.question: Which formats are commonly used for CloudFormation Templates?
What is a CloudFormation Stack?
options: A collection of AWS resources created and managed as a single unit from a template

questions.question: What happens when a CloudFormation Stack is updated?
options: CloudFormation compares the current and desired state and applies necessary changes
A new AWS account is created

questions.question: Why do organizations use CloudFormation?
options: To automate infrastructure deployment and maintain consistency across environments

questions.question: A company wants identical Dev, Test, and Production environments. Which AWS service can help achieve this?
What is a major benefit of Infrastructure as Code?
options: Version control, automation, and repeatable deployments

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes CloudFormation Templates and Stacks?
options: Templates define AWS infrastructure as code, while Stacks are the deployed instances of those templates that create and manage AWS resources
Templates and Stacks are identical concepts
Stacks are used only for monitoring

questions.question: What does High Availability (HA) mean in AWS?
options: Designing systems to remain operational even when components fail

questions.question: What is an Availability Zone (AZ)?
options: One or more isolated data centers within an AWS Region

questions.question: What is a Multi-AZ architecture?
options: Deploying resources across multiple Availability Zones within the same Region
Deploying resources in multiple AWS accounts
Deploying resources in multiple VPCs only

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of Multi-AZ deployments?
options: Improved fault tolerance and high availability

questions.question: What is a Multi-Region architecture?
options: Deploying resources across multiple AWS Regions
Deploying resources across multiple subnets only
Deploying resources across multiple IAM groups
Deploying resources in a single AZ

questions.question: Which disaster scenario is Multi-Region architecture designed to address?
options: Entire Region failure or large-scale disaster

questions.question: A company wants its application to survive the failure of one Availability Zone. Which architecture should it use?
A financial application requires business continuity even if an entire AWS Region becomes unavailable. Which architecture is most appropriate?
Which AWS service can help route users to healthy endpoints across Regions?
Which statement best summarizes Multi-AZ and Multi-Region patterns?
options: Multi-AZ provides high availability within a Region, while Multi-Region provides disaster recovery and resilience across Regions
Multi-AZ is only used for databases

questions.question: What is the primary goal of Disaster Recovery (DR)?
options: To restore systems and data after a disruption or disaster

questions.question: What is a backup?
options: A copy of data stored separately for recovery purposes

questions.question: What is the purpose of a restore operation?
options: To recover data or systems from a backup

questions.question: What does RTO stand for?
What does RPO stand for?
If a company has an RTO of 2 hours, what does it mean?
options: Systems must be restored within 2 hours after a disruption
Data must be backed up every 2 hours

questions.question: If a company has an RPO of 15 minutes, what does it mean?
options: Up to 15 minutes of data loss is acceptable

questions.question: Which AWS service is commonly used for storing backups?
Which disaster recovery strategy provides the fastest recovery but typically costs the most?
Which statement best summarizes RTO and RPO?
options: RTO defines acceptable downtime, while RPO defines acceptable data loss
RTO and RPO mean the same thing
RTO measures storage size and RPO measures bandwidth

questions.question: What is the AWS Well-Architected Framework?
options: A set of best practices and design principles for building secure, reliable, efficient, and cost-effective cloud architectures

questions.question: What is the primary goal of the AWS Well-Architected Framework?
options: To help teams build and operate high-quality cloud architectures
To increase EC2 instance size automatically

questions.question: How many pillars are included in the AWS Well-Architected Framework?
Which pillar focuses on protecting systems, data, and assets?
Which pillar focuses on recovering from failures and maintaining service availability?
Which pillar focuses on monitoring systems, automating changes, and improving operational processes?
Which pillar focuses on using resources efficiently and selecting the right technologies?
Which pillar focuses on avoiding unnecessary costs and maximizing business value?
Which pillar was added to help organizations reduce environmental impact?
Which statement best summarizes the AWS Well-Architected Framework?
options: It provides six architectural pillars that help organizations design secure, reliable, efficient, sustainable, and cost-optimized cloud solutions
It is used only for networking
It applies only to large enterprises

questions.question: What is the primary focus of the AWS Certified Solutions Architect – Associate (SAA-C03) exam?
options: Designing secure, resilient, high-performing, and cost-optimized AWS architectures

questions.question: Which AWS Well-Architected pillar is most closely associated with designing fault-tolerant systems?
Scenario: A company wants its application to remain available if an Availability Zone fails. Which solution is most appropriate?
options: Deploy all resources in a single AZ
Use Multi-AZ architecture with Load Balancing

questions.question: Scenario: An application experiences unpredictable traffic spikes. Which AWS service combination is best?
options: EC2 + Auto Scaling + Application Load Balancer

questions.question: Scenario: A company wants to store large amounts of static website content at low cost. Which AWS service is most suitable?
Scenario: A global application needs to deliver content with low latency to users worldwide. Which AWS service should be used?
Scenario: A company wants to reduce database read load while improving application response times. Which AWS service is most appropriate?
Scenario: A company needs a serverless architecture that runs code in response to events. Which AWS service should be selected?
Scenario: A security team wants to know who modified an IAM policy yesterday. Which AWS service provides this information?
Scenario: A company wants to automatically evaluate whether S3 buckets comply with encryption requirements. Which AWS service should be used?
What is an architecture trade-off in cloud design?
options: Choosing between different design options based on business requirements and constraints
Deploying resources in a single Availability Zone

questions.question: Why is it important to consider trade-offs when designing AWS architectures?
options: Every design decision affects cost, security, performance, or reliability
Security is the only factor that matters
Cost is always the highest priority

questions.question: Scenario: A company wants the lowest possible cost for a non-critical internal application. Which option is most appropriate?
Scenario: A financial application requires maximum availability and minimal downtime. Which design is most appropriate?
options: Multi-AZ deployment with Load Balancing and Auto Scaling

questions.question: Which design choice generally increases reliability but also increases cost?
Which AWS service helps improve security through centralized identity and access management?
Scenario: A company wants to improve security by enabling encryption for all stored data. What is the trade-off?
options: Slightly increased operational complexity while improving data protection

questions.question: Which AWS architecture provides the highest reliability but generally has the highest cost?
Scenario: A startup wants a balance between cost and availability. Which architecture is often recommended?
options: Multi-AZ deployment within a single Region

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes architecture trade-offs?
options: Cloud architects must balance security, cost, and reliability based on business requirements because optimizing one area often impacts another
Security and cost have no relationship
